question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. It immediately solved the problem and explained the solution as a monologue, with no engagement of the user at any decision point (sieve choice, bytearray vs list, p² start, slice-assignment idiom).

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all. The system prompt's two-option paradigm was entirely bypassed — no fork on sieve vs. trial division, no fork on data structure, no fork on the p² optimization.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding to evaluate; the answer was delivered in one shot with the numerical result up front, then a top-down explanation. The user had no opportunity to reason through any step.

flow_and_tone.rationale: The explanation is clear and accurate, but the 'flow' is a one-way lecture terminated by the user with `<done/>`. There is no back-and-forth, which is the core of the intended pair-programming dynamic.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
